name: Alabama Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Alabama — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

provider.name: Board of Dental Examiners of Alabama

address.description: 2229 Rocky Ridge Road, Vestavia Hills, AL 35216-9286

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

mainEntity.name: Does Alabama require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, Alabama requires Expanded Duty Dental Assistant through the state board.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in Alabama?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Alabama.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in Alabama?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Alabama?

acceptedAnswer.text: State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.
